title: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. (AAOI): A Bull Case Theory
content:
We came across a bullish thesis on Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. on Make Money, Make Time’s Substack by Oliver | MMMT Wealth. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on AAOI. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.'s share was trading at $34.47 as of January 14th. AAOI’s forward P/E was 84.03 according to Yahoo Finance.Lumentum (LITE) Hits New 52-Week High on Bullish Rating, Improved Q1 Earnings

asharkyu/Shutterstock.com

Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. (AAOI) is an emerging player in the optical communications space, positioned as a smaller but highly aggressive competitor to Coherent, Inc. (COHR). The company differentiates itself through speed and pricing, allowing it to rapidly gain market share. AAOI has recently secured major contracts with Microsoft, signaling strong validation of its technology and growth strategy. The company is experiencing explosive growth, with estimated revenue increasing 65% in FY26 and EBITDA expected to nearly double with 93% growth, reflecting operational leverage and scale benefits.

While AAOI is currently not profitable at the net income level, the company is projected to achieve positive earnings in FY26, underscoring its transition from high-growth investment to sustainable profitability. Valuation metrics are distorted due to the early-stage profitability profile: next-twelve-month (NTM) EV/Sales sits at 3.8x, while NTM EV/EBITDA and P/E ratios—51.4x and 183.6x respectively—are less meaningful given the limited earnings track record.

Despite these elevated multiples, the combination of rapid revenue growth, high-margin potential, and strategic contract wins with top-tier clients positions AAOI as a compelling growth story in the photonics sector. Investors are likely to view the company’s trajectory as one with substantial upside, particularly as it leverages new contracts to scale operations and improve profitability. While short-term earnings volatility may persist, the long-term narrative of aggressive expansion, technological differentiation, and market validation makes AAOI a standout opportunity among optical communications peers.

Previously, we covered a bullish thesis on Fabrinet (FN) by Industrial Tech Stock Analyst in April 2025, which highlighted the strategic Amazon warrant deal, signaling long-term partnership and diversification beyond Nvidia. FN’s stock price appreciated by approximately 139.72% since our coverage. Oliver | MMMT Wealth shares a similar perspective but emphasizes Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.’s (AAOI) rapid growth and major Microsoft contracts as key catalysts.

Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. is not on our list of the 30 Most Popular Stocks Among Hedge Funds. title: Applied Optoelectronics Reaches Analyst Target Price
content:
In recent trading, shares of Applied Optoelectronics Inc (Symbol: AAOI) have crossed above the average analyst 12-month target price of $27.20, changing hands for $29.10/share.  When a stock reaches the target an analyst has set, the analyst logically has two ways to react: downgrade on valuation, or, re-adjust their target price to a higher level.  Analyst reaction may also depend on the fundamental business developments that may be responsible for driving the stock price higher — if things are looking up for the company, perhaps it is time for that target price to be raised.

There are 5 different analyst targets within the Zacks coverage universe contributing to that average for Applied Optoelectronics Inc, but the average is just that — a mathematical average.  There are analysts with lower targets than the average, including one looking for a price of $15.00.  And then on the other side of the spectrum one analyst has a target as high as $34.00.  The standard deviation is $7.596.

But the whole reason to look at the average AAOI price target in the first place is to tap into a "wisdom of crowds" effort, putting together the contributions of all the individual minds who contributed to the ultimate number, as opposed to what just one particular expert believes.  And so with AAOI crossing above that average target price of $27.20/share, investors in AAOI have been given a good signal to spend fresh time assessing the company and deciding for themselves: is $27.20 just one stop on the way to an even higher target, or has the valuation gotten stretched to the point where it is time to think about taking some chips off the table? title: Western Digital Corporation (WDC): A Bull Case Theory
content:
We came across a bullish thesis on Western Digital Corporation on The Simple Side’s Substack. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on WDC. Western Digital Corporation's share was trading at $214.00 as of January 13th. WDC’s trailing and forward P/E were 29.88 and 28.09 respectively according to Yahoo Finance.Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) Climbs 20.6% on Hyperscaler Order

Copyright: ralwel / 123RF Stock Photo

Western Digital Corporation develops, manufactures, and sells data storage devices and solutions based on hard disk drive (HDD) technology in the United States, Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. The company offers internal HDDs, data center drives, data center platforms, external drives, portable drives, NAS for home and office, and accessories.

Western Digital Corp (WDC) presents a compelling investment case driven by disciplined execution, market leadership, and strategic focus on high-capacity nearline HDDs. In Q1FY26, the company reported $2.82 billion in revenue, a 27% year-over-year increase, with non-GAAP EPS of $1.78, a 30.4% operating margin, and $599 million in free cash flow.

Guidance for Q2FY26 anticipates sequential revenue growth to ~$2.9 billion with mid-40s margins, while a $0.125 dividend increase and $553 million in buybacks demonstrate strong cash conversion and confidence in the demand environment. Following the February 2025 separation of its Flash business, Western Digital now focuses exclusively on HDD operations, aligning cost structure with cloud-driven storage demand and providing clearer visibility into profitability and capital allocation.

The company’s diversified exposure across Cloud, Client, and Consumer markets, with cloud leading revenue and margin gains, supports resilient growth. Technological leadership is underscored by accelerated ePMR and HAMR qualifications, which improve yield, density, and gross-margin potential toward the high-40s.

Management maintains disciplined capex at 4%–6% of revenue, avoiding new unit capacity while sustaining pricing power. Multi-year orders from hyperscale customers and a robust backlog provide visibility into revenue and margins through CY27, supporting structural growth in a previously cyclical industry.

Valuation analyses using DCF, SOTP, and peer multiples indicate fair value in the high-$100s, with upside toward $250 if margin and technology milestones are met, while downside near $90 is mitigated by strong cash generation. WDC’s combination of operational discipline, technological innovation, and visible cash flows positions it as an attractive investment with asymmetrical upside potential.

Story Continues

Previously, we covered a bullish thesis on Seagate Technology Holdings plc (STX) by fortitudelkw in January 2025, which highlighted STX’s leadership in high-capacity HDDs and strong positioning in the AI-driven cloud storage market. The company's stock price has appreciated by approximately 194.36% since our coverage. This is because the thesis played out as AI and cloud demand surged. The Simple Side shares a similar perspective but emphasizes Western Digital’s disciplined execution, Flash separation, and multi-year order visibility supporting structural growth and margin expansion.

Western Digital Corporation is not on our list of the 30 Most Popular Stocks Among Hedge Funds. As per our database, 84 hedge fund portfolios held WDC at the end of the third quarter which was 74 in the previous quarter. While we acknowledge the potential of WDC as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.
